questions.question: 1. In a passband ASK system operating over an AWGN channel, which factor most significantly degrades its performance at low SNR?

explanations.a: Incorrect! Phase distortion affects PSK more than ASK.
explanations.b: Correct! ASK performance degrades rapidly at low SNR because amplitude is directly affected by noise.
explanations.c: Incorrect! ASK can be bandwidth efficient with proper filtering.
explanations.d: Incorrect! ASK can use coherent or non-coherent detection, but noise is a larger issue.

questions.question: 2. In coherent passband FSK systems, how is intersymbol interference (ISI) minimized?

explanations.a: Incorrect! Increasing deviation doesn’t inherently remove ISI.
explanations.b: Correct! Orthogonal frequencies ensure minimal overlap and eliminate ISI.
explanations.c: Incorrect! Filtering is more applicable in linear modulations like PSK.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Differential encoding addresses phase ambiguity, not ISI.

questions.question: 3. Compared to passband PSK, passband FSK is generally more robust in which type of communication channel?

answers.d: Flat-fading channels with strong phase jitter

explanations.a: Incorrect! PSK may perform better in pure AWGN with coherent detection.
explanations.b: Incorrect! FSK handles frequency selectivity better due to its spectral spread.
explanations.c: Incorrect! FSK can be more immune to narrowband impulsive noise.
explanations.d: Correct! FSK is superior under phase jitter because it doesn’t depend on phase.

questions.question: 4. Why is coherent detection preferred over non-coherent detection in passband PSK systems?

answers.b: It improves BER performance by recovering phase information.
answers.d: It makes the system more robust to frequency offset.

explanations.a: Incorrect! Coherent detection is more complex, not simpler.
explanations.b: Correct! Recovering phase allows optimal decision boundaries and lower BER.
explanations.c: Incorrect! Bandwidth is unaffected by coherent vs non-coherent detection.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Frequency offset tolerance is not the strength of coherent PSK.

questions.question: 5. What limits the minimum frequency separation in passband binary FSK for orthogonality?

explanations.a: Incorrect! Carrier phase does not affect frequency orthogonality.
explanations.b: Incorrect! Modulation index affects tone spacing, but the base constraint is symbol time.
explanations.c: Correct! Orthogonality in FSK is achieved when frequency separation = 1 / symbol duration.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Power doesn't impact orthogonality directly.

questions.question: 6. In passband ASK systems, what is the main reason it is rarely used in power-constrained wireless applications?

answers.b: It suffers from poor spectral efficiency.
answers.c: It has high peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR).
answers.d: It is highly susceptible to amplitude variations from fading.

explanations.b: Incorrect! Spectral efficiency can be decent depending on shaping.
explanations.c: Incorrect! ASK usually has lower PAPR than multilevel modulations.
explanations.d: Correct! Fading affects amplitude severely, degrading ASK signals.

questions.question: 7. For Binary PSK in a passband system, what is the theoretical BER expression over an AWGN channel with coherent detection?

explanations.a: Correct! Correct expression for BER in coherent BPSK over AWGN.
explanations.b: Incorrect! This is the expression for some non-coherent schemes.
explanations.c: Incorrect! This is equivalent to option a, but not in standard Q-function form.
explanations.d: Incorrect! This is an approximation, not exact BER.

questions.question: 8. What is the impact of Doppler spread on passband PSK systems in mobile channels?

explanations.a: Incorrect! Timing offset is less influenced by Doppler.
explanations.b: Incorrect! Doppler causes frequency spreading and phase shifts, not just amplitude fading.
explanations.c: Correct! Doppler introduces rapid phase changes, challenging phase tracking.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Doppler usually degrades performance unless counteracted.

questions.question: 9. In passband FSK systems, what is the role of the modulation index (h) in performance?

explanations.a: Incorrect! Timing is not controlled by modulation index.
explanations.b: Correct! h = Δf × Tb; controls frequency separation and impacts bandwidth/ISI.
explanations.c: Incorrect! Phase jitter is a PSK issue.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Amplitude fading resistance comes from frequency-based modulation itself.

questions.question: 10. For a passband PSK system under ideal coherent detection, what characteristic of the constellation helps minimize BER?

answers.a: Equal amplitude and orthogonal phase spacing

explanations.a: Correct! Equal amplitude and uniform phase spacing ensure maximized distance between symbols.
explanations.b: Incorrect! PSK does not vary amplitude.
explanations.c: Incorrect! Frequency separation applies to FSK, not PSK.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Pulse shaping reduces ISI, not BER directly under ideal detection.
